items.rationale: Long-held conviction that ZKPs are underappreciated and transformational; actively tracks privacy-on-Ethereum (Aztec) and L1 privacy (Aleo, Nym). Sees privacy as core to the cypherpunk mission.
items.age_note: Thesis stated as early as 2022-01-06 and continuously reaffirmed through 2023-05; treated as current given recent restatements.

citations.quote: i still think people underestimate how transformational zero knowledge proofs are
The next generation of Aztec is on the horizon, check out how privacy can work on Ethereum!
One small step for decentralization, one big step for privacy preserving technologies

items.rationale: Believes blockchains can provision real-world services (compute, storage, energy, connectivity). Has a crisp investment heuristic — networks work where verification cost is below incumbent margin.

citations.quote: Excited to be a part of @FundingCommons and to talk about decentralized physical infrastructure networks (DePIN)!
The essential question when considering building a DePIN network: Verification cost < incumbent margin

items.rationale: Wants to rebrand token mechanism design as 'Tokenology' and push tokens beyond financial use into broad societal coordination. Wrote two blog posts; this is his signature personal thesis.

citations.quote: tired: Tokenomics wired: Tokenology
Tokenomics ... inherently focuses on financial applications of blockchains, but I believe the societal ramifications can be much broader

items.rationale: Excited that anyone can contribute to a protocol/DAO and be retroactively compensated by value created; sees it as essential for DAO success, though open on how to evaluate contribution quality.
items.age_note: First raised 2022-07-29, restated with explicit excitement 2023-02-24; current.

citations.quote: I'm very excited about the idea of permissionless contribution.
anyone can proactively contribute to a dao and will retroactively receive compensation based on the value of their contribution

items.rationale: Most recent enthusiasm — backing decentralized ML infrastructure (Gensyn) and exploring ZK-proven inference and token-owned AI protocols.
items.age_note: Newest theme in the corpus (2023-02 onward), so freshest signal.

citations.quote: The future of machine learning is decentralized ✨
prove inference with validity proofs posted on Ethereum ... create a DAO to own/operate the protocol

items.rationale: Closely watches Ethereum's rollup-centric scaling and Cosmos convergence (L3s/hyperchains, mesh security, shared sequencers); intellectually engaged but probing on throughput.

citations.quote: History is rhyming in how L2s are being deployed on Ethereum
It seems L3s (also called hyper chains) can solve many of the same challenges as L1 appchains

items.rationale: Led a seed round in Entropy; believes MPC/smart-contract wallets can finally move past the self-custody-vs-security tradeoff and bring decentralization to custody.
items.age_note: Strong 2022-06 conviction (investment thesis); not restated through H1 2023, so persisted rather than fresh.

citations.quote: Entropy has the potential to bring decentralization to custody, allowing permissionless, secure, and efficient access to almost any token for any user.

items.rationale: Invested in Morpho; sees on-chain lending as an essential DeFi primitive and peer-to-peer matching atop pools as a Pareto rate improvement.
items.age_note: Core thesis from 2022-07; last touched 2022-11 (Instadapp integration). No 2023 restatement — persisted.

citations.quote: Morpho has the potential to significantly lower the cost of decentralized capital and increase capital efficiency across DeFi.

items.rationale: Argues truly decentralized games require on-chain logic, enabling emergent and cooperative win conditions; praised Dark Forest / Lattice.
items.age_note: Only voiced in early 2022; never restated. Older, persisted view.

citations.quote: if you want truly decentralized crypto games you have to put logic on-chain.

items.rationale: His most concrete worry: crypto networks have a centralization chokepoint at the networking layer where ISPs/DNS or a few RPC/storage providers could censor or fail to persist data.

citations.quote: centralization at the network level that would allow DNS providers and ISPs to censor large portions of most crypto networks
If only a few nodes actually store blob data it's centralizing

items.rationale: Considers 'MEV' a muddy term conflating user-harming extraction with benign activity, and treats eliminating harmful extraction (via markets or privacy) as an unsolved hard problem.

citations.quote: MEV is a bad term because it includes both actions that hurt users, and actions that are neutral or helpful

items.rationale: Repeatedly stresses decentralization is always less efficient than centralization; the cost must be justified, and unjustified complexity is itself a problem.

citations.quote: Decentralization is always less efficient than centralization.
Adding complexity without purpose becomes a problem unto itself

items.rationale: Open concern that permissionless contribution, reputation and peer-assessment systems can be biased by malicious parties — the verification problem he calls crypto's crux.

citations.quote: there is always the challenge that malicious parties could bias it
Decentralized verification is the hardest problem and crux of why crypto matters

items.rationale: Default platform in nearly all his recent thinking — privacy (Aztec), rollup scaling, ZK-proven inference settlement. Alignment is implicit rather than a price call.
items.age_note: Inferred from repeated platform references (2023-02 through 2023-06); he never states a token/price view.

items.rationale: Earlier praised Solana as the high-throughput, atomically composable execution environment for on-chain games; hosted Toly at CSS.
items.age_note: Specific endorsement is from 2022-01; later mentions (2023-04/06) are event-hosting, not a fresh thesis.

citations.quote: it would be great to see some teams chose @solana for the highest throughput atomically composable execution environment

risk_regime.rationale: Posture is structurally bullish and builder-optimistic on crypto's long-term transformation ('crypto is still so early', ETH Denver as a 'whitepill', explicit advice to 'indulge optimism'). This is ideological/long-horizon conviction, not a tactical view on token prices or market timing — he never comments on market levels, cycles as a trade, or near-term risk appetite.

notes: Wuollet is a thesis-driven early-stage investor/cypherpunk, not a markets/trading voice — he expresses essentially no tactical price or token-allocation views, so tokens_excited is thin and extrapolated, and tokens_concerned is empty. His durable through-line is decentralization + decentralized verification as crypto's reason-to-exist ('crypto replaces trusted third parties with decentralized verification mechanisms'). Freshest momentum (H1 2023) is toward Tokenology/token design, DePIN, and decentralized ML/AI (Gensyn); ZK/privacy is a stable high-conviction backbone. Older theses (Morpho/DeFi lending, Entropy/custody, on-chain games) are marked persisted, not reversed — no statement contradicts them; they simply weren't restated in 2023. No genuine reversals were found in the corpus; views compound rather than flip. Risk regime is inferred from consistent optimism, with low confidence because no market-timing statements exist.
